expectations.text: BallastComponent has PID gains (Kp, Ki, Kd) and BallastTanks with capacity/fill/rates
expectations.evidence: BallastComponent.java declares depthKp=2000f, depthKi=50f, depthKd=800f, depthIntegral, prevError, deadBand, and Array<BallastTank> tanks. BallastTank.java has capacity, currentFill, fillRate, drainRate, and localPosition fields.

expectations.text: BallastSystem implements PID controller: proportional + integral + derivative terms for depth error
expectations.evidence: BallastSystem.processEntity() computes error = targetDepth - currentDepth, accumulates depthIntegral += error * dt (clamped to [-10,10]), computes dError = (error - prevError) / dt, then command = depthKp * error + depthKi * depthIntegral + depthKd * dError (lines 99-106). All three PID terms are present.

expectations.text: Ballast mass changes update rigid body via setMassProps()
expectations.evidence: BallastSystem.processEntity() at lines 123-127 computes ballastMass = getTotalBallastMass(ballast), obtains inertia from body.getLocalInertia(), then calls physBody.body.setMassProps(hull.dryMass + ballastMass, inertia).

expectations.text: Hull crush depth triggers a HullBreachEvent via EventBus
expectations.evidence: BallastSystem.processEntity() at lines 130-137 checks if currentDepth > hull.crushDepth and calls eventBus.publish(new SubmarineHullBreachEvent(entity, currentDepth, hull.crushDepth)). HullComponent.java has crushDepth field (default Float.MAX_VALUE). SubmarineHullBreachEvent is a dedicated event class.

expectations.text: FloodingSystem uses Torricelli's theorem (flow proportional to sqrt of head) for breach ingress
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.processEntity() Phase 1 (lines 99-113) computes flow = BREACH_CD * comp.breachArea * sqrt(2 * g * comp.breachDepth), which is exactly Torricelli's theorem Q = Cd * A * sqrt(2*g*h). The Javadoc explicitly names Torricelli's theorem.

expectations.text: Cross-compartment flow equalizes based on water head difference between connected compartments
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.processEntity() Phase 2 (lines 119-147) iterates connected compartments, computes headDiff = waterHead(comp) - waterHead(neighbor), then crossFlow = PASSAGE_CD * PASSAGE_AREA * sqrt(2*g*abs(headDiff)) * signum(headDiff) * dt. Water is transferred from higher-head to lower-head compartment. The waterHead() method returns fill fraction as a proxy for head.

expectations.text: Free surface effect is computed for partially-flooded compartments
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.computeFreeSurfaceEffect() (lines 239-268) checks fillFraction between 0.01 and 0.99 (partial fill only), computes I_free = (1/12)*breadth*length^3 with partialFactor = 4*fillFraction*(1-fillFraction) peaking at 50% fill, then GZ_loss = rho * totalIFree / displacement. Triggers StabilityWarningEvent when GZ_loss exceeds threshold.

expectations.text: Flooding shifts the rigid body's centre of mass, not just its total mass
expectations.evidence: FloodingComponent stores floodedCoM and updateFloodedMassAndCoM() (lines 193-213) computes a mass-weighted centre-of-mass shift from compartment centroids. However, adjustRigidBodyMass() (lines 219-225) only calls setMassProps(totalMass, inertia) — it updates total mass but never applies the computed floodedCoM offset to the rigid body's transform or centre of mass. The btRigidBody's actual CoM is not shifted. The computed CoM is stored in the component but not applied to the physics body.
